headline: Oil surge and geopolitical tension split AI models on market direction
summary: A major disagreement emerged among AI models on 2026-03-09, with Claude and Perplexity predicting sharp declines across broad indices due to oil surge past $107 and weak jobs data, while GPT-4o and Grok leaned bullish on positive pre-market sentiment. The energy crisis narrative (Iran disruptions, Kuwait/Iraq cuts) created a clear divergence: bearish models expected ~2% SPY drops and stagflation pressures, while bullish models cited Asian/European strength and tech earnings optimism. Actual market closes remain pending, leaving the fundamental disagreement unresolved.

consensus_picks.note: Two models with strong conviction (0.82-0.85 confidence) predicted 2% declines from oil/jobs shock, opposed by two bullish models.
Both bearish models saw tech as more vulnerable to oil/bond yield pressures than broader market.
Both bullish models cited product momentum and positive social sentiment despite macro headwinds.

best_call.summary: Perplexity's down prediction on SPY had the highest bearish confidence (0.85) with precise futures data citing ESH26 lows and 2% declines, grounded in specific oil price and employment data.

worst_call.summary: GPT-4o's bullish QQQ target of 400 appears most at odds with market fundamentals, as it contradicts all four bearish models' tech-specific downside risk from stagflation pressures.
